units.title: What's in Your Classroom?
units.subtitle: Classroom objects and positions
grammar_focus: Prepositions of Place (in, on, under, next to, behind)

lessons.title: Objects
lessons.topic: A tour of the school computer room
lessons.context: A tour of the school computer room.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use a computer for?
questions.hint: Think about what you can do when you sit at a computer!

questions.question_text: What can you find in a computer room?
questions.hint: A computer room is a special place at school. What do children sit on and use there?

questions.question_text: What do you click with when you use a computer?
questions.hint: It's small and you hold it in your hand. It helps you point and click!

items.definition: A table you use for work or study.
items.example: My books are on the desk.

items.definition: A piece of furniture to sit on.
A flat surface for writing or drawing.
A machine for playing games and more.
You sit at this to write and work
You type and click on this

steps.title: Our Computer Room
steps.text: Today we visit the computer room. There is a big desk next to the window. A black computer is on the desk. My chair is under the desk. The white board is behind me.
steps.scene_description: elementary school computer lab with children sitting at desks with computers, bright and colorful classroom

steps.explanation: We use these special words to tell where things are!
examples: The computer is on the desk.
The chair is next to the board.
The book is under the desk.

items.correctSentence: The computer is on the desk
The chair is under the desk
The board is behind me
My book is next to the computer
The pen is in the bag

items.sentence: The computer is ___ the desk.
The chair is ___ the table.
The cat is ___ the box.
The board is ___ the teacher.
My bag is ___ the chair.
The computer is at the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things on top of surfaces

items.sentence: The chair are under the desk.
My book is in next to the computer.
The bag is under desk.
Two computer is on the table.
The book is on the desk.
The chair is at the table.
items.corrected: The chair is next to the table.

items.sentence: The computer is behind the board.
The pen is to the book.
items.corrected: The pen is next to the book.

items.sentence: The cat is under the chair.

items.question: The book is ___ the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things on top of surfaces

items.question: The computer is ___ the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'next to' for things beside something

items.question: The board is ___ me.
items.explanation: Use 'behind' for things at the back

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Welcome to our computer room. Look, there are ten computers on the desks. Can you see the big white board behind me? Good! Now, please sit on your chairs next to the desks. Let's start our computer lesson!

questions.question: How many computers are in the room?

steps.prompt: Look at your classroom and describe where things are!
steps.expected_text: The computer is on the desk

prompts.prompt: Say: The computer is on the desk.
prompts.expected_text: The computer is on the desk

prompts.prompt: Say: The chair is next to the desk.
prompts.expected_text: The chair is next to the desk

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: Where is the board in your classroom?
prompts.expected_text: The board is behind the teacher

questions.question_text: Which one is a computer?
The book is ___ the desk.
Where do you sit in the computer room?
True or False: The teacher writes on the board.
The bag is ___ the chair.

lessons.title: Positions 1
lessons.topic: Finding a lost pencil in the classroom
lessons.context: Finding a lost pencil in the classroom.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do you keep your pencils at school?
questions.hint: Think about what you use to carry your pencils and crayons!

questions.question_text: Oh no! You can't find your pencil. Where should you look first?
questions.hint: Where were you sitting when you last used it?

questions.question_text: Your pencil fell down. Where is it now?
questions.hint: When something falls, it goes down to the floor!

items.example: The cat is in the box.
The book is on the table.
The cat is under the table.

items.sentence: The pencil is ___ the pencil case.
The book is ___ the table.
The cat is hiding ___ the bed.
My lunch is ___ my bag.
The cup is ___ the desk.
The ball rolled ___ the chair.
The crayons are ___ the box.
The paper is ___ the table.

steps.title: Where Is My Pencil?
steps.text: Emma can't find her pencil. She looks on her desk. It's not there. She looks in her pencil case. It's not there. Then she looks under her chair. There it is!
steps.scene_description: young girl searching for pencil in classroom, looking under desk and chair

questions.question: Where does Emma find her pencil?
Is the pencil in the pencil case?

steps.explanation: We use 'in' for inside something, 'on' for on top of something, and 'under' for below something.
examples: The pencil is in the bag.
The book is on the table.
The ball is under the chair.

items.correctSentence: The pencil is in the bag
The book is on the desk
The cat is under the table
My ruler is on the chair
The crayons are in the box

items.sentence: The pencil is ___ the pencil case.
The book is ___ the table.
The ball is ___ the chair.
My bag is ___ the desk.
The cat is hiding ___ the bed.
The pencil are in the box.
The book is at the table.
items.explanation: Use 'on' when something is on top of a surface

items.sentence: My pencil is under the desk and I see them.
items.explanation: Use 'it' for one thing, 'them' for many things

items.sentence: She have a pencil in her bag.
Two book are on the desk.
items.explanation: Add 's' to make plural when there are two or more

items.sentence: The cat can sleeps under the bed.
items.explanation: After 'can' we use the base form of the verb

items.sentence: The pencil is in the bag.
The book is at the table.
items.corrected: The book is on the table.
items.explanation: Use 'on' when something is on top of a surface

items.sentence: The cat is under the chair.
My crayons is in the box.
items.corrected: My crayons are in the box.

items.sentence: The ruler is on the desk.

items.question: Where is the pencil? It's ___ the bag.
My pencils ___ in the pencil case.
The ruler is ___ the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'on' when something is on top of a surface

steps.audio_text: Oh no! I can't find my pencil. Let me look on my desk. No, it's not there. Is it in my pencil case? No, not there either. Wait! I can see it under my chair. There it is!

questions.question: Where does the speaker look first?
Is the pencil in the pencil case?

steps.prompt: Practice saying where things are in your classroom!
steps.expected_text: The pencil is in the bag.

prompts.prompt: Say: The pencil is in the bag.
prompts.expected_text: The pencil is in the bag.

prompts.prompt: Say: The book is on the desk.
prompts.expected_text: The book is on the desk.

prompts.prompt: Say: The ruler is under the chair.
prompts.expected_text: The ruler is under the chair.

questions.question_text: The pencil is ___ the bag.
Which word means 'below something'?
Where is the book? It is ___ the table.
True or False: 'The cat is under the chair' means the cat is on top of the chair.
Emma can't find her pencil. She looks on her desk and in her pencil case. Then she finds it under her chair. Where was the pencil?

lessons.title: Positions 2
lessons.context: Describing where pictures are hanging in the room.

steps.instruction: Let's think about pictures in our classroom! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do we usually see pictures in a classroom?
questions.hint: Look around! Pictures are high up where everyone can see them!

questions.question_text: What can you see in classroom pictures?
questions.hint: Think about the pictures in your classroom. What helps you learn?

questions.question_text: How do pictures stay on the wall?
questions.hint: Pictures don't move! They stay in one place on the wall!

items.example: The ball is behind the chair.
The cat is next to me.

items.definition: The bottom part of a room.
items.example: The toy is on the floor.

steps.title: Our Classroom Pictures
steps.text: Look at our classroom! There is a big picture on the wall. It is next to the window. My drawing is behind the teacher's desk. There are some books on the floor next to my bag.
steps.scene_description: colorful elementary classroom with pictures on walls, children's drawings, teacher's desk, and school bags on floor

questions.question: What is next to the window?
Where are the books?
questions.answer: on the floor next to the bag
options: on the floor next to the bag
questions.correct_answer: on the floor next to the bag

steps.explanation: We use special words to tell WHERE things are in our classroom!
examples: The picture is on the wall.
The picture is next to the door.
The picture is behind the teacher's chair.

items.correctSentence: The picture is on the wall
My bag is under the desk
The cat is behind the door
The book is next to the pen
The ball is in the box

items.sentence: The picture is ___ the wall.
The chair is ___ the table.
The dog is ___ the bed.
My toy is ___ the box.
The clock is ___ the door.
The picture is in the wall.
items.explanation: We say 'on the wall', not 'in the wall'

items.sentence: My books is on the floor.
The cat are under the chair.
Her is next to the window.
Two picture are on the wall.
items.explanation: Add 's' for two or more things

items.sentence: The picture is on the wall.
items.corrected: The picture is on the wall.

items.sentence: My bag is in the floor.
items.corrected: My bag is on the floor.
items.explanation: We say 'on the floor', not 'in the floor'

items.sentence: The books are next to the pen.
items.corrected: The books are next to the pen.

items.sentence: The cat is behind door.
items.corrected: The cat is behind the door.

items.sentence: The toy is under the bed.
items.corrected: The toy is under the bed.

items.question: The picture is ___ the wall.
items.explanation: We use 'on' for things attached to walls

items.question: My pencil is ___ my book.
items.explanation: Use 'next to' when something is beside another thing

items.question: The chair is ___ the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'behind' when something is at the back

items.question: The books are ___ the floor.
items.explanation: We use 'on' for things resting on flat surfaces

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let's look at our classroom pictures today. There is a big alphabet picture on the wall next to the board. Can you see the animal pictures? They are behind my desk. Oh, and please pick up the crayons on the floor next to your bags!

questions.question: Where is the alphabet picture?
questions.answer: on the wall next to the board
options: on the wall next to the board

questions.question: What pictures are behind the teacher's desk?
Where are the crayons?
questions.answer: on the floor next to the bags
options: on the floor next to the bags

steps.prompt: Look around your room. Tell me where you see pictures!
steps.expected_text: The picture is on the wall.

prompts.prompt: Say: The picture is on the wall.
prompts.expected_text: The picture is on the wall.

prompts.prompt: Say: My picture is next to the window.
prompts.expected_text: My picture is next to the window.

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: Where is a picture in your room?
prompts.expected_text: The picture is on the wall next to my bed.

questions.question_text: The picture is ___ the wall.
Which word means 'beside something'?
Where do we walk in the classroom?
True or False: 'Behind' means something is at the back.
My bag is ___ the door. I can't see it!

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Find the object challenge using prepositions
lessons.context: Find the object challenge using prepositions.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where is the pencil in this picture?
questions.hint: Look at the desk. What do you see on top of it?

questions.question_text: The book is under the table. Where is it?
questions.hint: Listen carefully! The sentence tells you where the book is!

questions.question_text: Look! The ball is in the box. Where can you find the ball?
questions.hint: The word 'in' means inside something. Where is the ball?

items.definition: You sit here to write and do your work
You can type and play games on this

items.question: Where do you sit to do your work?
The bag is _____ the desk. You can't see it!
The ruler is _____ the book. They are together.
You walk on this in the classroom.

steps.title: Where Is My Pencil?
steps.text: Emma is looking for her pencil. She looks on her desk. It's not there. She looks under her chair. No pencil! Then she looks in her bag. Yes! Her pencil is in her bag next to her book.
steps.scene_description: young girl searching for pencil in classroom, looking in school bag

questions.question: What is next to the pencil?

steps.explanation: We use special words to show where things are in our classroom.
examples: The pencil is on the desk.
The bag is under the chair.
The book is in the box.
The ruler is next to the pen.
The ball is behind the door.

items.correctSentence: The book is on the desk
The cat is under the chair
The ball is in the box
The computer is next to the wall
The bag is behind the door

items.sentence: The pencil is ___ the desk.
The cat is ___ the table.
The book is ___ the bag.
The chair is ___ the desk.
The ball is ___ the door.
The picture is ___ the wall.
The book is at the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things on top of a surface

items.sentence: The cat is next the chair.
The ball are in the box.
The pencil is under the wall.
items.explanation: Things go 'on' the wall, not 'under' it

items.sentence: Two book is on the desk.
The cat is under the chair.
The book is in the desk.
items.corrected: The book is on the desk.
items.explanation: Books usually go 'on' the desk, not 'in' it

items.sentence: The ball is behind the door.
The computer is next the wall.
items.corrected: The computer is next to the wall.

items.sentence: The pencil is on the floor.

items.question: The picture is ___ the wall.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things attached to the wall

items.question: The ball is ___ the door.
items.explanation: Use 'behind' for at the back of something

items.question: The computer is ___ the desk.
items.explanation: Use 'on' for things on top of a surface

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let's play a game. I'm looking at something in our classroom. It's on the wall next to the board. It's a clock! Now you try. Can you find something under your desk?

questions.question: Where is the clock?
questions.answer: on the wall next to the board
options: on the wall next to the board

steps.prompt: Look around your room. Tell me where things are!
steps.expected_text: The book is on the desk.

prompts.prompt: Look at your desk. Say: The book is on the desk.
prompts.expected_text: The book is on the desk.

prompts.prompt: Look under your chair. Say: The bag is under the chair.
prompts.expected_text: The bag is under the chair.

prompts.prompt: Now tell me about something in your room. Say: The [something] is [in/on/under/next to/behind] the [something].
prompts.expected_text: The pencil is in the box.

questions.question_text: The pencil is ___ the desk.
Where do you sit to do your work?
True or False: 'The cat is under the chair' means the cat is below the chair.
Read: 'The ball is behind the door.' Where is the ball?
questions.correct_answer: at the back of the door
options: at the back of the door

questions.question_text: The book is ___ the bag. (You can't see it!)
